title: Now’s the time to book that Cape Cod getaway
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Cape Cod summer rental bookings for July and August are already 65% filled, prompting experts to recommend booking soon.
Conde Nast Traveler recently named Cape Cod as a top destination for Memorial Day weekend getaways.
Blake Decker, CEO of Pretty Picky Properties, advises travelers to book Cape Cod vacations early to secure accommodations.

title: Charlie McAvoy’s reward as Bruins’ OT hero? Another trip to the dentist’s chair.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the overtime winner in the Bruins' 2-1 victory over the Los Angeles Kings.
Despite injuries, McAvoy has excelled, helping the Bruins maintain a playoff spot with his seventh goal of the season.
McAvoy required dental work post-game after suffering facial injuries but returned to play and secure the win.

title: After her son was struck by a car, she’s running the Boston Marathon for Newton firefighters
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Maureen Murphy, 43, from Newton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Newton Firefighters Children's Fund.
Murphy's son, Teddy, was struck by a car two years ago, and Newton firefighters provided essential medical care.
Funds raised will support children of fallen firefighters across the Commonwealth, as a thank you to first responders.

title: Victor Wembanyama shines, Jaylen Brown is ejected as Celtics drop chaotic loss to Spurs: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Victor Wembanyama scored 39 points, leading the Spurs to a 125-116 win over the Celtics in San Antonio.
Jaylen Brown was ejected in the first half for confronting an official, impacting the Celtics' scoring options.
Derrick White led the Celtics with 34 points, showcasing defensive and offensive prowess against the Spurs.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington home at 353 Plymouth St., built in 1900, sold for $555,000 with 3 bedrooms on a 26,553 sqft lot.
Allston's 22 Royal St., a two-family home built in 1900 with 9 bedrooms, sold for $1,202,000.
Boston's 46 Chestnut St. historic row-middle home, built in 1900, sold for $22,000,000 with 6 bedrooms.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy's overtime goal secured the Bruins' 13th straight home win, defeating the Los Angeles Kings 2-1.
Bruins defenseman McAvoy suffered a facial injury during the game, requiring immediate follow-up dental care.
McAvoy's resilience and performance are noted as potential for leadership amid the Bruins' current captain vacancy.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected with 3:38 left in the second quarter after two technical fouls in a game against the Spurs.
Brown criticized referees on social media following his ejection, posting a message on X expressing frustration.
The Celtics lost 125-116 to San Antonio, missing Brown, Payton Pritchard, and Nikola Vucevic due to his ejection and injuries.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of Margarita Morehead, 59, in Chelsea in 2023.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder and other charges, violating a protection order against Morehead granted in 2022.
Prosecutors described Alvarez's attempt to evade police and his eventual decision to turn himself in later that evening.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The alleged assault occurred on February 27 at 8 p.m. in the Prudential station on the Green Line.
The suspect allegedly spat on and punched the victim multiple times, according to MBTA Transit Police.
Police describe the suspect as wearing black clothing with a hoodie, short hair, beard, and tattoos on neck and hands.

title: Maine man allegedly shot and killed girlfriend; pair worked together at ski resort
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Austin Doucette, 24, was charged with murder after allegedly shooting his girlfriend Makayla Rose DeSantis, 23, in Carrabassett Valley, Maine.
DeSantis was shot at a condo complex used by Sugarloaf Mountain Ski Resort employees and died later in a Portland hospital.
Doucette, held without bail, was arrested Monday night following a warrant from Farmington District Court, Maine Police reported.
